stories.title: Macron praises Europe’s predictability in face of countries that ‘hurt you without even informing you’ – as it happened
stories.summary: This live blog is now closed Meanwhile, the European Union has sought to ramp up pressure on Hungary to drop its veto on the €90bn loan for Ukraine, with the European Commission saying it will push ahead with its preparatory work for the loan to be paid out. The commission said it would draft a legal text setting out the details of the first payment of €45bn in 2026 and what the funds would be used for, and send it to the European Council to be formally approved by the bloc’s 27 leaders. “ We pr

stories.title: The Guardian view on Ukraine’s perilous spring: Europe’s steadfast support is more vital than ever | Editorial
stories.summary: As the world’s attention is drawn to the Middle East, Donald Trump’s assault on Iran has had damaging knock-on consequences for Kyiv After meeting European Union foreign ministers this week, Volodymyr Zelenskyy voiced exasperation over the continued blocking of a €90bn EU loan to Kyiv by Hungary’s prime minister, Viktor Orbán. US financial support for Ukraine has dried up under Donald Trump, so the money is desperately required. But as Mr Zelenskyy bitterly observed, it is being delayed “because

stories.title: Peter Magyar, the former Orban ally vying for power in Hungary
stories.summary: Magyar represents the biggest threat to Viktor Orban's rule in Hungary since he won the first of four consecutive victories in 2010.

stories.title: Clearing up the scandal that has cast suspicion on the Hungarian secret service is entirely up to the government
stories.summary: It has been days since Direkt36 published an article about an unusual police and intelligence operation, after which an investigator came forward and claimed that his unit had been pressured by the Hungarian intelligence services to conduct house searches at the homes of two IT specialists linked to the Tisza Party. There are no signs of the scandal abating any time soon, and the information which has come to light so far has become highly politicized. One of the IT specialists involved in the c

stories.title: Viktor Orbán has the support of both Russia and the US. Here’s why that doesn’t seem to be doing him any good | Péter Krekó
stories.summary: In Hungary, the Iran war is exposing tensions. On April 12, voters may decide that Orban’s geopolitical contortions are a liability On 3 March, Viktor Orbán held a phone conversation with Vladimir Putin. According to official Hungarian reporting, the discussion focused on “energy issues” and other routine matters. What followed was anything but routine. Within days, the Hungarian foreign minister, Péter Szijjártó , had flown to Moscow, and returned with two freed prisoners of war , dual citizens

stories.title: ‘God squad’ waives endangered species law to allow US drilling in Gulf of Mexico
stories.summary: Critics say exemption for fossil fuels exploits White House’s ‘self-made gas crisis’, and could doom the rare Rice’s whale Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox A US government panel on Tuesday exempted oil and gas drilling in the Gulf of Mexico from the Endangered Species Act (ESA), a move which critics say could doom a rare whale species and harm other marine life . The Endangered Species Committee – which had not convened in more than three decades – vo

stories.title: Ezekért az előnyökért tényleg érdemes új építésű lakást vásárolni! (x)
stories.summary: Mitől lesz egy új építésű lakás valóban jó választás – az elhelyezkedéstől, a műszaki tartalomtól vagy attól, hogy milyen életet lehet benne élni? A hamarosan költözhető, Duna-parti Waterside Residence esetében mindhárom tényező kiemelkedő. Megnéztük, mit kínál ma az egyik legizgalmasabb új budapesti lakópark-fejlesztés.

stories.title: Magyar tervezésű hajlítható játékkonzol érdekel? (x)
stories.summary: Még mielőtt mindenki rohanna a boltokba, nézzünk a naptárra, és mondjuk ki magunkban: április elseje. De azért játsszunk el egy kicsit a gondolattal, hogy miért ne robbanthatná be egy új magyar találmány a játékkonzolok piacát? Hiszen megannyi világhírű találmány kötődik magyar tudósokhoz a biztonsági gyufától, a töltőtollon keresztül a telefonközpontig. Jó szórakozást a következő néhány percre, és ajánljuk további böngészésre a cikk végén elhelyezett részletes tájékoztató oldalát is!

stories.title: Angliában szívbetegségek megelőzésére is ajánlják a népszerű súlycsökkentő szereket
stories.summary: Azoknak a betegeknek, akiknek a BMI-je 27 felett van, és akiknél magas a szív- és érrendszeri betegségek kockázata.

stories.title: Az oroszbarát tyituski módszer: keménykötésű figurákat küldtek rá az utcán az ellenzékre
stories.summary: Orbán országjárásán is megjelentek azok a kormányközeli kötődésű embereknek, akiknek az ellenzéki hangok elnyomása a feladata. A jelenség emlékeztet arra, ahogy a Janukovics-rezsim emberei, a tyituskik léptek fel az ellenzékkel szemben Ukrajnában, de nálunk nem történt olyan erőszak, ami az oroszbarát rendszerekben általános volt.

stories.title: Leszedik a választási plakátokat a budapesti hidak szerkezetéről
stories.summary: Oszlopokra lehet plakátolni, csak a hidak szerkezeti elemeire nem.

stories.title: Rákosrendezőn 2,5-szer annyi lakás épülhet, mint tavaly Budapesten összesen
stories.summary: A most beígért 10 ezer új lakás jelentősen javíthatná az elmúlt évek siralmas és romló statisztikáit.

stories.title: Moszkva reagált Szijjártó és Lavrov kiszivárgott hangfelvételére
stories.summary: Az orosz külügyi szóvivő örült annak, hogy közzétették a felvételt.

stories.title: Árulónak nevezett Pintér Sándor? Nem biztos, hogy tudja, az állomány mit gondol – Szabó Bence az elmúlt napokról
stories.summary: Miért lepte meg, hogy hogyan fogadták a rendőrségen? Mit gondol arról, hogy Orbán baleknak nevezte? Interjú a Tisza elleni műveletről borító Szabó Bencével.

stories.title: Undorítónak nevezte Szijjártó és Lavrov beszélgetését az ukrán külügyminiszter
stories.summary: Andrij Szibiha nyomozás megindítását sürgette az ügyben.

stories.title: Ukraine and Russia react to leaked Szijjártó-Lavrov calls
stories.summary: Ukraine's Foreign Minister Andriy Sybiha used the words "disgusting" and "disgrace", but the Russian Foreign Ministry's Maria Zakharova had a different opinion.

stories.title: Féltik a szavazataikat: több erdélyi Tisza-szavazó személyesen viszi a borítékját Magyarországra
stories.summary: Egyre többen döntenek úgy Erdélyben, hogy nem adják át szavazatukat az RMDSZ-es közvetítőknek, hogy biztosak legyenek benne, nem vész el vagy nem módosul útközben.

stories.title: Az ír és a német nyelvű irodalom körül forog majd minden a 31. Budapesti Nemzetközi Könyvfesztiválon
stories.summary: Az idei fesztivál díszvendége az ír Colm Tóibín lesz, és a Frankfurti Könyvvásár is csatlakozik a rendezvényhez.

stories.title: „Semmit nem csinálnék máshogy” – mondta a Tisza informatikusai elleni nyomozásról kitálaló Szabó Bence
stories.summary: Az NNI volt nyomozója reagált arra, hogy Orbán Viktor baleknak, Pintér Sándor pedig árulónak nevezte. Azt is elárulta, hogy NAV-os nyomozóként találkozott a költségvetési csalás miatt elítélt Hajdú Péterrel, akit még gyanúsítottként hallgatott ki, és aki most költözött be egy adás erejéig Orbán Viktorhoz.

stories.title: „Harmadosztályú trash celeb, bűnöző” – Hajdú Péter is szóba került Magyar Péter szeghalmi beszédében
stories.summary: A Tisza Párt elnöke arra reagált, hogy Szabó Bence a Telex élő műsorában elmondta, a NAV nyomozójaként dolgozott a műsorvezető korábbi adócsalási ügyén.

stories.title: Nem feltétlenül kell leállítani a Barátság-vezetéket ahhoz, hogy felújítsák
stories.summary: Működő vezeték mellett is lehet szivattyút cserélni – állítják a hazai szakemberek. Az, hogy az ukránok hajlandóak költeni a Barátság-vezetékre, azt jelzi, hogy hosszú távon is terveznek vele.

stories.title: A Chelsea a Premier League történetének legnagyobb veszteségét hozta össze az előző szezonban
stories.summary: 65 millió fonttal szárnyalták túl a Manchester City eddigi rekordját, pedig állításuk szerint a bevételük is majdnem rekordmagas lett.

stories.title: A fideszes jelölt csak ámul és bámul, Csepel exfideszes polgármestere mégis neki hálás mindenért
stories.summary: A kampányban saját, közpénzzel is támogatott civil szervezetét is bevető Király Nóra még azon is meglepődött, hogy Csepelen van mozi. Persze hivatalosan is csak annyi köze van a kerülethez, hogy ő lett ott a Fidesz képviselőjelöltje. A polgármester mégis mindent neki köszön, legyen az új Lidl, felújított rendelő, vagy akadálymentes játék a Süsü játszótérre.

stories.title: Orbán Viktor a fiataloknak Szentesen: Esetleg meg is köszönhetnétek
stories.summary: Kisebb, fiatalokból álló tiltakozócsoport gyűlt össze a beszéd alatt, akik skandálással és dudálással zavarták meg Orbán Viktor beszédét. A miniszterelnök többször is hozzájuk intézte a szavait.

stories.title: „Ilyenek az ukránok”, kampányolt Szijjártó az egyik legszegényebb választókerületben
stories.summary: Ami egyben az egyik legfideszesebb is. A külügyminiszter Baranya aprófalvas választókerületben, a 4-es számúban erősítette a helyi jelölt kampányát.

stories.summary: Ex-TV host pledged to centre party around equity, with higher wealth taxes, green energy and tuition-free education Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox Canada’s embattled New Democratic party (NDP) has elected the former broadcaster and self-proclaimed socialist Avi Lewis as its new leader, as it looks to rebuild following a devastating federal election last year that saw it lose official party status. stories.title: In an Istanbul market, I found an old German phrase book – and a reminder of how not to speak to migrants | Carolin Würfel
stories.summary: Turkish immigrants to Germany in the 60s were seen as temporary labour, not people. Today’s government in Berlin is at risk of repeating the mistake A few weekends ago, I went to the flea market in Bomonti, a neighbourhood on the European side of Istanbul. I go there regularly, and over the years I’ve accumulated a small collection of things: embroidered napkins, records, old issues of House & Garden, earrings, candle holders. It is usually on the days when you are not looking for anything i

stories.title: Starmer calls for ‘ambitious’ new UK-EU ties as Trump threatens to quit Nato
stories.summary: PM to focus on European defence and economic partnership for ‘dangerous world’, in pivot away from US UK politics live – latest updates The UK will seek an even deeper partnership with the EU because of the instability wreaked by Donald Trump’s war with Iran, Keir Starmer has said, adding that the moment called for a more ambitious deal with Brussels to strengthen trade and defence. His comments came as the US president again said he was considering pulling the US out of Nato , which he describe

stories.title: Hundreds rally for birthright citizenship at supreme court: ‘We are an immigrant nation’
stories.summary: Some underscore Trump’s unprecedented court appearance as protesters defend 14th amendment right Around 250 demonstrators packed the steps of the supreme court on Wednesday, chanting in defense of birthright citizenship as Donald Trump himself watched from the public gallery in an unprecedented appearance. Beija McCarter, an eighth grade US history teacher, and Noah Goldstein, a New Yorker who was also at last month’s trans rights rally, both arrived at the demonstration with little optimism abo

stories.title: Cambodia: Alleged cyber scam boss extradited to China
stories.summary: Authorities say Li Xiong was arrested in Cambodia and deported at the request of Chinese authorities. He is linked to an accused scam center kingpin who was extradited in January.

stories.title: Spain hits back at Trump in escalating row over euthanasia
stories.summary: Spain blasted the U.S. government on Wednesday for probing a high-profile euthanasia case in Barcelona, kicking off a diplomatic rift over human rights and end-of-life laws. The dispute was spurred by a leaked U.S. diplomatic cable ordering the American Embassy in Madrid to open an investigation into Spain’s decision to allow sexual assault victim Noelia Castillo to undergo legal euthanasia last week . After suffering repeated sexual assaults and surviving a suicide attempt that left her paralyz

stories.title: Trump, Stubb had ‘constructive’ NATO talk with alliance’s future in the balance
stories.summary: Finnish President Alexander Stubb, one of Donald Trump’s closest European confidants, said he and the U.S. leader had a “constructive discussion” on Wednesday about NATO and problem solving. Stubb’s remarks come as Trump publicly mulls pulling the United States out of the transatlantic alliance , with Western leaders nervously anticipating a national address from Trump scheduled for 9 p.m. local time, which is the middle of the night in Europe. In a post on X Stubb wrote: “Spoke with

stories.title: FBI declares suspected Chinese hack of US surveillance system a ‘major cyber incident’
stories.summary: The FBI last week deemed a recent, China-linked cyber intrusion into a sensitive agency surveillance system a “major incident,” meaning it poses significant risks to U.S. national security, according to one congressional aide and two U.S. officials with knowledge of the matter. The bureau  first told Congress  on March 4 that it was investigating suspicious activity on an internal agency system that contained “law enforcement sensitive information.” The FBI did not publicly identify wh

stories.title: Prominent UK pro-Palestine activists guilty of breaching protest conditions

Ben Jamal, 62, the director of the Palestine Solidarity Campaign (PSC), and Chris Nineham, 63, vice-chair of the Stop the War Coalition, were accused of failing to comply with conditions im

stories.title: Oil price falls and markets rally after Trump says Iran war over in ‘two to three weeks’
stories.summary: US president’s claim that conflict is nearing end prompts 15% drop in Brent crude and stock market climb in Asia Business live – latest updates Oil prices tumbled and stock markets have rallied across the world after Donald Trump said the war in Iran would end in “two to three weeks”. Brent crude, the international benchmark for oil, fell as low as $98.35 a barrel on Wednesday, down more than 15% on the previous day and its lowest level in a week. stories.title: Swedish PM offers deal that could see far-right allowed into government
stories.summary: Party, which has neo-Nazi roots, will hold ‘important ministerial posts within immigration’ if four-party coalition wins in September The Swedish prime minister, Ulf Kristersson, has said that he will allow the far-right Sweden Democrats (SD) into government for the first time – and give its members key ministerial posts – if his coalition wins the next general election. Despite becoming Sweden’s second biggest political party after the Social Democrats in the last election, SD currently plays o

stories.title: Trump says war in Iran will not end until Strait of Hormuz is reopened
stories.summary: President Donald Trump said Wednesday that the war with Iran would not end until the Strait of Hormuz is reopened, as global leaders brace for an extended disruption to the energy market. “Until then, we are blasting Iran into oblivion or, as they say, back to the Stone Ages!!!”  he wrote on Truth Social on Wednesday . The president’s post — a seemingly new condition for ending the war — comes as he  plans to deliver a prime time speech  to “provide an important update on Iran” on

stories.title: Trump lashes out at Nato: will Europe stand up to him? - The Latest
stories.summary: Donald Trump has said he is considering pulling the US out of Nato, likening the alliance to a ‘paper tiger’. It comes after weeks of denouncements from the US president against allies for not helping to reopen the strait of Hormuz. When asked about Trump’s comments, Keir Starmer said: ‘Nato is the single most effective military alliance the world has ever seen’ and ‘whatever the noise, I’m going to act in the British national interest in all the decisions that I make’. stories.summary: Cannabis policy still divisive two years in, with SPD hailing it while CDU minister says it is risk to young people’s health It was a landmark piece of legislation passed by Germany’s previous, centre-left-led government: a measure that legalised the personal recreational use of cannabis for over-18s despite warnings from critics it would cause a steep rise in the drug’s use, including by teenagers, and boost criminal gangs. Two years on, controversy over the move has still not been stubbed out,

stories.title: ‘System malfunction’ causes robotaxis to stall in the middle of the road in China
stories.summary: Distressed riders who were stranded for hours say Apollo Go customer service agents offered ‘useless platitudes’ A “system malfunction” has caused several self-driving robotaxis to stall in the middle of the road in China, police have confirmed, after distressed riders were stranded for hours. stories.title: CPS advising UK police on investigation into Andrew and Mandelson links to Epstein

The former duke of York and the former UK ambassador to the US were both arrested in February on suspicion of misconduct in public office over their connections with the late financier. stories.title: Resident doctors accuse Keir Starmer of sabotaging talks to end pay and jobs dispute
stories.summary: British Medical Association leaders say PM’s threat to axe posts makes strike action more likely Resident doctors have accused Keir Starmer of damaging the prospects of a deal to end their pay and jobs dispute by threatening to axe 1,000 new jobs for medics in the NHS. The claim from the British Medical Association leaders came just before the Thursday deadline given by the prime minister for the union to accept the government’s final offer. Continue reading...

stories.summary: Fifty-seven people were killed when two trains collided in 2023 in the worst rail accident in Greek history, with 36 accused in a trial expected to last years.

stories.summary: Trump has criticised Nato members for showing a lack of support for US objectives in Iran.

stories.summary: Iran's strikes on Gulf states have been especially hard for migrants who have long supported these economies to lift their families back home from poverty.

stories.title: Swiss finance minister files criminal charges over remarks generated by Elon Musk’s Grok
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — Swiss finance minister Karin Keller-Sutter has sued for defamation and insult over remarks generated by Elon Musk’s artificial intelligence assistant. The liberal politician had been the subject of a March 10 post on X that included alleged vulgar remarks created by Grok, the chatbot developed by xAI. stories.title: ‘Fatal decision’: EU slammed for caving to US pressure on digital rules

But instead of creating a sovereign path forward for Europe, [Commission President Ursula] von der Leyen kisses the ring time and again,” Greens la

stories.title: American journalist Shelly Kittleson kidnapped in Iraq, US officials say
stories.summary: State department says it is working to ensure release of freelancer ‘as soon as possible’ after abduction in Baghdad Middle East crisis – live updates An American journalist, Shelly Kittleson, has been kidnapped in Baghdad by a suspected Iranian-backed Iraqi armed group, the US has said, as regional security deteriorates after the US-Israeli attack on Iran. stories.title: In Europe, lobbyists are using soaring fuel prices to make the case for more dirty energy

stories.title: Reeves criticises Trump for starting Iran war with no ‘clear plan’ to get out of it – as it happened

stories.title: Parents told to ‘take responsibility’ after two days of TikTok-led London disorder
stories.summary: Six teenage girls arrested after hundreds of young people gather in Clapham in ‘swarming the streets’ trend Police have urged parents to “take responsibility” after scenes of widespread disorder in Clapham, south-west London, on Saturday and Tuesday. Officers said the incidents were caused by a TikTok trend for swarming the streets. Six teenage girls have been arrested so far, and the Metropolitan police said there would be more arrests in the coming days as officers reviewed CCTV and body worn

stories.title: UK is most vulnerable European country to jet fuel shortages, Ryanair boss says
stories.summary: Michael O’Leary says UK’s reliance on Kuwait for jet fuel supply amid Iran war exposes it to possible shortages The UK is the most vulnerable country in Europe to potential jet fuel shortages as the Iran war throttles supplies from the Gulf, the boss of Ryanair has said. Michael O’Leary, the chief executive of the budget airline, said Britain would be the most exposed to jet fuel shortages because it relies on Kuwait for about 25% of its supply. Continue reading...

stories.title: UK food inflation ‘could hit 9%’, trade body warns as Reeves meets retail chiefs
stories.summary: Discussion on how to ease impact from Iran war coincides with Food and Drink Federation almost tripling forecast Food inflation could hit 9% in the UK this year even if the strait of Hormuz opens within the next few weeks, figures suggest, as the Iran war pushes up energy prices. The Food and Drink Federation (FDF), which represents 12,000 food and drink manufacturers, has predicted prices will rise by “at least” 9% by the end of 2026, almost tripling a forecast of 3.2% that was made before the

stories.title: Artemis II, Nasa’s first crewed lunar rocket in more than half a century, prepares for launch – watch and follow live
stories.summary: Follow latest updates, including how to watch the launch, as four astronauts prepare to set off on a 10-day, 685,000-mile journey with millions watching The Artemis II launchpad stack comprises Nasa’s Space Launch System (SLS) rocket, and the Orion crew capsule , a five-meter diameter craft with the interior volume similar to that of a small camper van. The height of the rocket assembly is 322ft (98m), slightly higher than the Statue of Liberty (305ft), and London’s Elizabeth Tower, commonly kno

stories.title: US approves new oral weight-loss pill developed by Eli Lilly
stories.summary: Called Foundayo, the tablet becomes the second one to receive FDA’s green light after Novo Nordisk’s Wegovy The US Food and Drug Administration (FDA) gave the green light on Wednesday to a new oral weight-loss medication developed by the Indianapolis-based pharmaceutical company Eli Lilly. Known as orforglipron or brand name Foundayo, the once-daily tablet becomes the second GLP-1 drug in pill form to hit the market in a short span of time, arriving after Novo Nordisk’s Wegovy pill received appr

stories.title: Florida signs proof of citizenship law as Trump’s Save Act flounders in Senate
stories.summary: Florida is third state to pass proof-of-citizenship voting laws this year as red states enact provisions of federal bill Governor Ron DeSantis of Florida signed legislation on Wednesday to require documented proof of citizenship to register to vote and to begin a process that will eventually unenroll voters who have not provided citizenship documentation. Florida is now the third state to pass proof-of-citizenship laws for voting this year, after South Dakota and Utah’s governors each signed pro

stories.title: Asia ramps up use of dirty fuels to cover energy shortfall triggered by Iran war
stories.summary: South Korea will delay the shutdown of coal-fired plants, while the Philippines also plans to boost the output of its coal-burning plants Governments across Asia are ramping up their use of coal, the dirtiest fossil fuel, as they try to cover huge energy shortfalls triggered by the US-Israel war on Iran. The move has triggered warnings from climate experts who point to coal’s devastating environmental impact, and say the energy crisis should be a wake up call for governments to invest in renewab

stories.title: Migrant boat capsizes in Turkey leaving 18 dead while 21 were rescued
stories.summary: Turkish authorities say the vessel attempted to evade its Coast Guard before sinking. At least 18 people drowned in the incident, 21 were rescued and an unknown number were missing.

stories.title: Germany's chancellor said 80% of Syrians should return to their home country in the next three years. Syrians in Germany are outraged.
stories.summary: According to Chancellor Friedrich Merz, 80% of Syrians living in Germany are to return to their home country within the next three years. The chancellor's remark has drawn fierce criticism.

stories.title: Iran war: Strikes on steel deal painful blow to economy
stories.summary: The US-Israeli strikes on Iran's two biggest steel mills will likely harm Tehran's ability to wage war, but could also cripple Iran's economy in irreversible ways.

stories.title: Undiagnosed TB pose challenge for South Africa, Mozambique
stories.summary: Africa is making progress in reducing tuberculosis (TB). But experts say missed cases and drug-resistant infections are complicating the fight, particularly in South Africa and Mozambique.

stories.title: Is Pakistan setting up a buffer zone in Afghanistan?
stories.summary: Tensions on the Afghanistan-Pakistan border are escalating amid claims of a Pakistani "security zone" and civilian casualties. Will calls for a ceasefire have any affect?

stories.title: Germany: Conservatives call cannabis legalization 'a nonstarter' as 2-year report is filed
stories.summary: Conservatives see a two-year report on Germany's partial legalization of cannabis as evidence that it's been a failure. While the findings may be more nuanced, researchers did identify several problems.

stories.title: World Cup 2026: Iraq qualify but Iran doubts loom
stories.summary: Iraq have overcome tough circumstances to reach the World Cup for the first time in 30 years, with the lineup now complete. But Iran's potential withdrawal still hangs over a tournament fraught with political issues.

stories.title: Germany: Hope fades for stranded humpback whale's survival
stories.summary: Local authorities have announced that they will cease efforts to guide a young humpback whale to its home waters. Now a restricted area has been established to allow it to "die in peace."

stories.title: NASA's Artemis II set to take off for moon voyage
stories.summary: Artemis II will bring humans back close to the moon for the first time since 1972 and mark the farthest humans have ever traveled in space.

stories.title: Court orders Trump to halt White House ballroom construction
stories.summary: The judge said US presidents are not owners of the White House and Congress must approve any changes to the building.

stories.title: US judge blocks Trump order that barred funding for NPR, PBS
stories.summary: A federal judge has blocked a presidential directive that barred funding for NPR and PBS media entities. Separately, an appeals court panel paused a federal judge's order to bring VOA employees back to work.

stories.title: Thousands lose their jobs in deep cuts at tech giant Oracle
stories.summary: It is thought that thousands of people may have lost their jobs at Oracle, one of the world's largest tech companies.

stories.title: China is trying to play peacemaker in the Iran war - will it work?
stories.summary: It's a turnaround for Beijing, whose official response has been muted so far. Why is China stepping in now?

stories.title: Israel intensifies Lebanon attacks and hits areas not in Hezbollah's control
stories.summary: Attacks have continued as Israel has announced its intention to control swathes of south Lebanon.

stories.title: Iran war economic shocks will last 'months', says Australia's PM
stories.summary: Anthony Albanese used a rare TV address to tell Australians that the "months ahead may not be easy" amid the global effects of the war.

stories.title: Brazil judge blocks Sugarloaf Mountain zipline
stories.summary: The project to build a zipline linking the popular Rio tourist spot to Morro da Urca proved controversial.

stories.title: Mass robotaxi malfunction halts traffic in Chinese city
stories.summary: Baidu has not responded to a request for comment about the outage, which affected at least 100 cars.

stories.title: Devastated Italians reckon with 'third apocalypse' of World Cup failure
stories.summary: Azzurri fans and management alike are pondering what went wrong after Italy fail to qualify for third World Cup in a row.

stories.title: DR Congo declares national holiday after reaching World Cup for first time in 52 years
stories.summary: This is just the second time that DR Congo have reached the World Cup finals, sparking celebrations in Kinshasa.

stories.title: Explosions at Burundi ammunition depot kill civilians, witnesses say
stories.summary: Powerful blasts destroyed homes and sparked panic in the city of Bujumbura on Tuesday night.

stories.title: Trump to give primetime address on war as questions swirl over his next move
stories.summary: The US president finds himself under growing pressure at home to avoid a protracted conflict.

stories.title: UK’s Reeves ‘angry’ over Trump’s decision to attack Iran
stories.summary: LONDON ― U.K. Chancellor Rachel Reeves said she is “angry” that U.S. president Donald Trump decided to attack Iran, which increasingly looks to be a serious threat to her goal of improving public finances. “I’m angry that Donald Trump has chosen to go to war in the Middle East — a war that there’s not a clear plan of how to get out of,” Reeves told BBC Radio 2 in an interview Wednesday. The invasion has rapidly increased inflationary pressures, with the Bank of England holding

stories.title: Germany: Conservatives link immigration with crime
stories.summary: Will German citizens feel safer if more immigrants are deported? Chancellor Friedrich Merz has been giving that impression. But the reality is more complex.

stories.title: Israel says it will keep control over part of southern Lebanon after war with Hezbollah ends
stories.summary: Defence Minister Israel Katz also says houses in Lebanese villages near the Israeli border will be demolished.

stories.title: Japan allows divorced couples to negotiate joint custody of children for first time
stories.summary: The new rules are the first major change to the country’s laws governing child-rearing in more than a century Divorced couples in Japan will be able to negotiate joint custody of their children from Wednesday, in the first major change to the country’s laws governing child-rearing in more than a century. Previously, Japan’s Civil Code required couples to decide which parent would take custody of their children when they divorce . Continue reading...

stories.title: Stephen Lewis, Canadian politician and social activist, dies aged 88
stories.summary: Lewis’s son Avi Lewis was elected leader of progressive New Democratic party a day before his father’s death Stephen Lewis, the Canadian diplomat, politician and human rights advocate, who spent decades tirelessly working to focus global attention on the HIV/Aids epidemic, has died of cancer. Lewis, who served as the Canadian ambassador to the United Nations, as well as the head of Ontario’s New Democratic party (NDP), was 88. Continue reading...

stories.title: Molly the border collie rescued after a week waiting for injured owner in New Zealand’s remote backcountry
stories.summary: A rescue mission involving volunteer helicopter crew and public donations ended in joy after Molly was located and brought home A spot of furry black and white appears among the jagged rocks of New Zealand’s alpine backcountry. It is Molly the border collie, sitting near the foot of a waterfall where she had been separated from her owner one week earlier. Molly was rescued on Tuesday after an avalanche of donations from the public funded a volunteer team made up of former helicopter pilots and c

stories.title: Finnish up! Claire Aho’s colour revolution – in pictures
stories.summary: The pioneering Nordic artist brought wit, verve and cinematic flair to postwar photography. A new exhibition celebrates her vibrant visual style Continue reading...

stories.title: Tiger Woods 'stepping away' for treatment after DUI arrest
stories.summary: Tiger Woods has announced a break from golf four days after he crashed his SUV and was arrested for driving under influence. Fans had been awaiting his return at the Masters this month.

stories.title: India news: Jet fuel prices reach record high amid Iran war
stories.summary: The Indian government has stepped in to limit the impact of record-high jet fuel prices on airlines and air fares. Commercial LPG cylinders have also gotten more expensive.

stories.title: Germany news: New fuel price law prompts huge midday spike
stories.summary: Filling stations responded to the new limit of one daily price hike with a hefty 7.5 cent average increase. Meanwhile, the BND spy agency confused workers by announcing an extra holiday on April Fool's day. DW has more.

stories.title: US journalist Shelly Kittleson kidnapped in Baghdad
stories.summary: The US state department says a suspect in her abduction has ties to an Iran-backed militia group, Kataib Hezbollah.

stories.title: 'A million things could go wrong' - why seizing Iran's uranium would be so risky for the US

stories.title: 'You're no longer my sister' - rows erupt as war divides Iranian families

stories.title: Judge temporarily halts construction of Trump's White House ballroom
stories.summary: The Republican-appointed judge ruled that Trump was a "steward" of the White House, not the owner.

stories.title: 'Moonshots' and merch - what it's like to be on Florida's Space Coast ahead of Artemis launch
stories.summary: Huge crowds are gathering around the Kennedy Space Centre to see the launch of the historic moon mission.

stories.title: Billion-plus people, three million officials, 33 questions - India begins huge census

stories.title: 'My six-year-old has nosebleeds': Chiang Mai air pollution sparks health fears
stories.summary: It's peak fire season in the Thai city, which now ranks as one of the world's most polluted.

stories.title: Divorced couples in Japan can now share custody of their children
stories.summary: Japan was, until recently, the only G7 country to not recognise the legal concept of joint custody.

stories.title: Megan Thee Stallion taken to hospital during Moulin Rouge performance
stories.summary: The rapper "started feeling very ill" mid-way through the show in New York, her representative told US media.

stories.title: 'It's phenomenal!': Excitement in Florida ahead of Artemis launch
stories.summary: BBC’s Leyla Khodabakhshi speaks to locals and visitors in Florida ahead of the Artemis II mission blast off.

stories.summary: Csütörtök délután jár le a határidő a nemzetiségi listára való jelentkezéshez is.

stories.title: A tüdőrák korai felismerésében a leghatékonyabb Krausz Ferencék felfedezése
stories.summary: A Nobel-díjas fizikus arról beszélt Szegeden, hogy a legfontosabb mindig a jó kérdés feltevése, és ez a tudományra különösen igaz. Az ő kérdése az volt, miért legyen kutató, a válasz pedig adta magát.

stories.title: A flat white-tól a narancsborig: a KÜNST egész napra kinyit (x)
stories.summary: A kilencedik kerület gasztrotérképén lassan két éve stabil pont a KÜNST Bistro, amely most szintet lépett. Márciustól nem kell megvárni a délutáni nyitást, hiszen hétfőtől szombatig már reggel nyolctól beülhetünk, ugyanis megnyílt a specialty coffee részlegük! Az esti műszakhoz hasonlóan kedves, elhivatott személyzettel találkozunk, ezért – és a hely adottságai miatt is – a környék otthon dolgozó lakóinak ajándék ez a reggeli nyitás.

stories.title: Kvóták, postások, buszok – az RMDSZ mindent megtesz, hogy szállítson két mandátumot a Fidesznek
stories.summary: Miközben az RMDSZ hivatalosan csak „segít” a levélszavazatok célba juttatásában, a színfalak mögött kőkemény mozgósítási gépezet zakatol. Forrásaink szerint megyékre lebontott számbeli elvárások, a postásoktól „elkerekített” listák és szervezett buszos utazások szegélyezik az utat a konzulátusokig. Mi áll a feltétel

stories.title: Trump erősen fontolgatja, hogy kiléptesse az Egyesült Államokat a NATO-ból
stories.summary: Az amerikai elnök erről azután beszélt, hogy külügyminisztere közölte, fel kell tenni a kérdést, hogy „miért is vagyunk a NATO-ban”.

stories.summary: Mi is elhittük, hogy Roznár Levente beszáll Puzsérék sorozatába, de az egész csak áprilisi poén volt.

stories.summary: Szabó Bence a Telex élő műsorában azt mondta, tapasztalatai szerint ez azt jelenti, hogy kevésbé fontos most az ő ügye, ezért döntött így a Központi Nyomozó Főügyészség.

stories.title: Összetört a Trump tömeges deportálásának arcává vált Kristi Noem a férjéről készült fényképek miatt
stories.summary: A Trump tömeges deportálását levezénylő miniszter férjéről kiderült, hogy hatalmas műmelleket hordva beszélgetett fétismodellekkel.

stories.summary: Hajdú 2020-ban kapott felfüggesztett börtönt költségvetési csalás miatt.

stories.summary: Az ügyészség friss közleményéből az is kiderült, hogy a férfi által vezetett Aston Martin majdnem a megengedett sebesség duplájánál, 93 km/h-nál sodródott ki.

stories.title: Kárpátalján találat ért egy alállomást, orosz drón először jutott ilyen messzire
stories.summary: Az áramhálózat alállomását érte orosz dróntalálat szerdán. A kár nem jelentős, de beszédes, hogy a fronttól ennél távolabbra eddig csak rakéta jutott Kárpátalján a háború négy éve alatt.

stories.title: Nem kell megszelídíteni, hiszen épp az a jó benne, hogy sírunk tőle
stories.summary: Az üveges tormák elveszítik az erejüket, illatukat, ízüket, de sokan félnek a friss tormától, pedig az ennek az ízesítőnek a legigazibb, legjobb formája. Miért érdemes inkább frissen megvenni, és mit kell kezdeni vele?

stories.summary: Sőt, a legutóbbi hatósági ellenőrzésen azt állapították meg, hogy tovább bővítették.

stories.summary: A továbbiakban az Európai Bizottság, a Tanács és a Parlament munkatársai sem használhatják így a mesterséges intelligenciát.

stories.title: Hajdú Péter szerint Szabó Bence állítása bizonyítja, hogy a rendőrség és a NAV is jól végzi a dolgát a Fidesz-kormány alatt

stories.title: Menczer nem hisz a kormányközeli Nézőpontnak, de azt elismerte, még 7-8 ezer szavazóra van szüksége
stories.summary: Tizenegy nap alatt kell meggyőznie őket.

stories.summary: A gyanú szerint a rendőr pénzért cserébe osztott meg nem nyilvános adatokat a két magánnyomozóval.

stories.title: At least 70 people killed and 30 injured in Haiti gang attack
stories.summary: Nearly 6,000 people forced to flee, human rights group says, as it criticises ‘abandonment’ from authorities At least 70 people have been killed and 30 injured during an attack in Haiti’s breadbasket Artibonite region, significantly more than official estimates, a human rights group has said. Police initially reported 16 dead and 10 injured, while a preliminary report from civil protection authorities suggested 17 had died and 19 were wounded. Continue reading...

stories.title: Sad faces all round as Bolivia’s clowns protest over decree threatening their livelihoods
stories.summary: Clowns in Bolivia are upset by mandate that stops schools hosting events from which they earn a living Dozens of clowns have marched through the streets of Bolivia’s capital to protest against a government decree that limits extracurricular activities in schools, threatening their livelihoods. Wearing full face paint and their signature red noses, the clowns gathered on Monday in front of the ministry of education in La Paz to oppose a decree published in February. The new mandate says schools m

stories.title: Pakistan and China propose five-part peace plan for Middle East
stories.summary: Foreign ministers Ishaq Dar and Wang Yi met in Beijing as Pakistan pushes for peacemaker role Middle East crisis – live updates Pakistan and China have released a joint five-part proposal for peace in the Middle East, after Pakistan’s foreign minister flew to Beijing on Tuesday to seek Chinese support for the country’s faltering efforts to negotiate an end to end the war. The one-day meeting between Ishaq Dar and his Chinese counterpart, Wang Yi, came as Pakistan continues to push for the role o

stories.title: Serbian local elections: Is Vucic's party losing its grip?
stories.summary: Serbia's ruling SNS once again secured victories in local elections — but sometimes just by a few hundred votes. Narrowing margins suggest a shifting political landscape and growing momentum for anti-government protests.

stories.title: Spheres of influence: the Bauhaus’s radical female photographers – in pictures
stories.summary: The images are famous, but the women who took them are often forgotten. An inspiring exhibition focuses on the pioneering ‘new vision’ of Marianne Brandt, Lucia Moholy and more Continue reading...

stories.title: Germany beat Ghana to complete confidence-boosting March
stories.summary: A late winner from Stuttgart's Deniz Undav secured a second friendly win in a week for Germany. With the FIFA World Cup just 10 weeks away, Julian Nagelsmann's team will be starting to believe they can win it all.

stories.title: After the Iran war, how fast could global trade recover?
stories.summary: The Iran war could end soon or drag on for much longer. Whenever the Strait of Hormuz is reopened, the true test will be how quickly energy, fertilizer and other supply chains can bounce back.

stories.title: Were Grandma and Grandpa Nazis?
stories.summary: What did our forebears do during the Nazi era? A subject long shrouded in silence in German families. Now, the US National Archives has made millions of NSDAP membership cards available online.

stories.title: Mediterranean sharks are vanishing in a legal void
stories.summary: Along Libya's coast, sharks — including breeding females — are sold unchecked. With little enforcement and growing financial pressure on fishermen, vulnerable species are spiraling toward extinction.

stories.title: After sex abuse claims, activists and lawmakers rethink Cesar Chavez Day
stories.summary: California has renamed Cesar Chavez Day to Farmworkers Day after the labour-rights icon was accused of sexual abuse.

stories.title: How Pakistan won over Trump to become an unlikely mediator in the Iran war

stories.title: Who are the Artemis II astronauts heading to the Moon?

stories.title: Shock, sadness and relief in town at centre of Australia's seven-month manhunt for Dezi Freeman

stories.title: Germany hosts Syria's Al-Sharaa despite human rights issues
stories.summary: Germany is keen for Syrian refugees to return home. The visit by interim Syrian President al-Sharaa highlights shifting policies on repatriation and regional stability — despite accusations of human rights violations.

stories.title: Finnish police say stray Ukrainian drone carried warhead
stories.summary: Finnish authorities are investigating two drones that crashed in southern Finland. Kyiv apologized for the incident, with Ukraine's Zelenskyy saying he and Finland's Stubb are on the same page.

stories.title: Urgent action needed to prevent surge in digital violence in Africa, experts say
stories.summary: A huge rise in internet users under the age of 30 has fuelled an increase in online violence against women and girls with devastating real-life effects, activists say Activists and lawyers in Africa are calling for urgent action to protect women, girls and boys as digital violence surges across the continent. A massive rise in internet users, coupled with huge numbers of people aged under 30 , has fuelled an increase in gendered online violence across the continent, according to experts, by givi

stories.title: Weather tracker: Thunderstorms drench UAE and Saudi Arabia
stories.summary: Abnormally strong jet stream triggers deluge in Middle East, while north Africa braces for 60-80mph gusts An unusual weather pattern unleashed severe thunderstorms across parts of the Middle East last week, battering countries including the United Arab Emirates and Saudi Arabia. The Arabian peninsula – typically dominated by arid desert climates – received up to 150mm of rain in just a few days. The deluge was caused by an abnormally strong jet stream, which helped a deep area of low pressure to

stories.title: UK politics: Trump says UK’s aircraft carriers are just ‘toys’ – as it happened
stories.summary: The comments were part of a broader address in which he condemned Nato allies Yesterday the Conservative party said that it wanted to ban political parties from distributing campaign literature in a foreign language. Announcing a plan to propose an amendement to the representation of the people bill to make this law, the shadow communities minister Paul Holmes said: Campaigning in a foreign language as the Greens did in Gorton and Denton only fosters greater division. A coherent national culture

stories.title: Amnesty flags FIFA World Cup human rights risks
stories.summary: The human rights group has sounded the alarm about risks faced by visitors to the 2026 FIFA World Cup. It goes as far as to describe the situation in the United States as a "human rights emergency."

stories.title: How US 'Liberation Day' tariffs reshaped global trade
stories.summary: One year after Trump's "Liberation Day" tariffs, global trade flows have shifted. Trade data shows which countries gained, which lost — and who is footing the bill.

stories.title: The pleasure of books in the digital age
stories.summary: The digitization of global literature has brought vast libraries to our fingertips. But the quiet power of a physical book offers a rare escape from constant digital noise.

stories.title: Germany: Stranded humpback whale moving again
stories.summary: Having barely budged the previous night, the whale stranded off Germany's Baltic coast for a week finally moved again late Monday. But after showing signs of deterioration, experts aren't sure where it's going next.

stories.title: How to fix Germany's costly health care system
stories.summary: A special commission has presented a raft of proposals aimed at curbing Germany's spiraling health care costs. But whether the government can impose them is another matter.

stories.title: Air Canada CEO to retire after English-only condolence furor
stories.summary: Air Canada has announced that CEO Michael Rousseau will retire later this year. This follows public criticism of his failure to voice condolences in French as well as English after two pilots were killed in a collision.

stories.title: First stop, the Moon. Next stop, Mars? Why Nasa's mission matters
stories.summary: From a race with China to lunar discoveries, the US is investing time, effort and money to head to the Moon - and beyond.

stories.title: How Australia’s seven-month-long manhunt came to an end
stories.summary: Fugitive Dezi Freeman has been wanted since he shot dead two police officers on his rural property in August last year.

stories.title: Inside Nasa's moon spacesuit lab ahead of Artemis launch
stories.summary: The Artemis II spacesuits are designed to protect the astronauts during launch and landing, as well as possible emergency scenarios.

stories.title: KP Sharma Oli: Nepal’s former prime minister arrested over alleged role in deadly protest crackdown
stories.summary: At least 77 people killed in anti-corruption youth uprising in September, which began over a brief social media ban Nepal’s former prime minister KP Sharma Oli was arrested early on Saturday morning over his alleged role in the deaths of dozens of people who took part in the gen Z protest that toppled his government last year. Police detained the three-time former prime minister at his residence in the capital Kathmandu, and also arrested his former home affairs minister Ramesh Lekhak. Continue

stories.title: Russian students duped into signing contracts with military
stories.summary: University students are being recruited to serve in drone units fighting Ukraine. They are offered lucrative contracts and promised they won't be sent to the front. Reports suggest those promises are not always kept.

stories.title: Finland reports drone crashes, alleges territorial violation
stories.summary: Two unidentified drones crashed in southern Finland. According to the country's prime minister, the drones may have been Ukrainian drones that strayed into Finnish airspace.

stories.title: Belgium's state-owned railway shines light on Holocaust role
stories.summary: A Holocaust survivor and the son of a Nazi collaborator have shared their experiences at Brussels' Train World museum. Talking to students, they warned of the dangers of forgetting history.

stories.title: France: Two new arrests over foiled Bank of America attack
stories.summary: French police arrested two more suspects over a foiled attack on Bank of America's Paris offices. The custody of the first arrested suspect arrested has been extended.

stories.title: Russia wants to legalize cars stolen in EU
stories.summary: Vehicles stolen in the EU may soon be legally registered in Russia. One German police union fears this could lead to an increase in car thefts in Europe.

stories.title: Hundreds in Beirut mourn journalists killed in Israeli strike
stories.summary: BBC reports from the funerals of three journalists killed by a targeted attack in southern Lebanon.

stories.title: Nepal’s PM-to-be uses rap to call for unity in first post-election message
stories.summary: Balendra Shah, 35, is a symbol of change in country whose government was toppled last year in youth-led uprising Nepal’s rapper turned politician Balendra Shah, who is about to be sworn in as prime minister, has issued his first post-election message in the form of a rap urging unity. Hours before the release he swore an oath as a newly elected lawmaker, and he is due to become the Himalayan republic’s new prime minister on Friday. Continue reading...

stories.title: Solar is winning the energy race
stories.summary: The world's cheapest power source is scaling at warp speed, pushing coal, gas and nuclear aside.

stories.title: Pope Leo tells Monaco to use 'gift of smallness' for good
stories.summary: Pope Leo XIV decried the widening gap between the rich and poor during a visit to the tiny European principality. He called on the ultra-wealthy residents of the tax haven to channel their prosperity toward justice.

stories.title: Whale stranded off Germany's coast gets stuck again
stories.summary: The unlucky humpback was spotted stranded on a sandbank in the Bay of Wismar, less than two days after being set free.

stories.title: Nestle says thieves stole 12 tons of KitKat chocolate bars
stories.summary: "We've always encouraged people to have a break with KitKat," the company said, "but it seems thieves have taken the message too literally."

stories.title: Zelenskyy visits Doha as Ukraine, Qatar sign defense pact
stories.summary: Ukraine and Qatar have agreed to cooperate on high-tech projects and share battlefield expertise on countering missile and drone threats.

stories.title: German public sector keen to end reliance on US tech
stories.summary: Government agencies depend on US-based servers and software, making them potentially vulnerable to political pressure and even blackmail. How can Germany achieve digital sovereignty?

stories.title: Germany news: Prognosis 'not good' for humpback whale
stories.summary: Hopes that a whale stranded in shallow waters off the Baltic coast can be guided back to the ocean are fading. Meanwhile, hundreds escaped from a nightclub that became engulfed in flames.

stories.title: UK police question suspect after Derby car incident
stories.summary: Seven people were seriously injured when a car plowed into pedestrians in the central English city of Derby. A man, who is originally from India, was arrested on suspicion of attempted murder.

stories.title: Pope Leo condemns using God to justify war in Palm Sunday mass
stories.summary: The pope has said God rejects the prayers of those who wage war and have "hands full of blood." Leo has also prayed especially for Christians in the Middle East during a Palm Sunday Mass.

stories.title: Watch: Iconic global landmarks turn off lights for Earth Hour
stories.summary: France's Eiffel Tower and Barcelona's Sagrada Família Basilica were among the famous global landmarks that went dark for an hour on Saturday night.

stories.title: ‘This is the saddest moment’: families search for loved ones on Eid after Kabul hospital strike
stories.summary: At least 400 killed in Pakistan’s strike on drug rehab centre, Taliban say, with families searching unmarked mass graves Sohrab Faqiri spent Eid, the Muslim festival to mark the end of the fasting month of Ramadan, looking for the grave of his brother, killed in a massive Pakistan airstrike on Kabul this week . Pakistan’s bombardment campaign, on what it says is terrorist and military infrastructure in neighbouring Afghanistan, appeared to have gone catastrophically wrong. A rehabilitation centr

stories.title: ‘We need to think much bigger’: trade minister calls for greater ambition in UK-EU reset
stories.summary: Exclusive: Chris Bryant says policy agreements are being done in bits and pieces but a greater vision is needed by both sides It was all smiles and warm handshakes when the two men in charge of renegotiating the UK’s relationship with the EU met in Brussels this week. Maroš Šefčovič and the UK minister for EU relations, Nick Thomas-Symonds, sharing a stage on the third floor of the vast European parliament building, were at pains to show the cross-Channel relationship was in a good place after y

stories.title: ‘We consider every mile we drive’: how fuel shortages are affecting readers worldwide
stories.summary: From a shop owner in India to a community worker in New South Wales, rising fuel prices are forcing people to ration oil usage Middle East crisis – live updates Alagesan, 35, needs liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) to run his roadside drink and snack shop in Coimbatore, India, but with the fuel shortage since the US-Israel attacks on Iran, he worries his business could fold. “I am far away from the Middle East, but my life is affected,” he said. “The gas cylinder is not available because of the war.

stories.title: Taliban release US academic held in detention for more than a year
stories.summary: Marco Rubio welcomes release of Dennis Coyle, who was detained in January last year for violating unspecified laws Afghanistan’s Taliban authorities have released the American academic Dennis Coyle after holding him for over a year, with the foreign ministry saying the release came on the occasion of Eid al-Fitr, the Muslim holiday that marks the end of the Islamic holy month of Ramadan. A statement from the ministry said the academic researcher had been released in Kabul on Tuesday, following a
